TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua za se Kapayuanan a ɫa Kadaladalavan.
S.PHON: izua za sə kapajuanan a ʎa kadaɭadaɭavan.
S.FORM: izua za se Kapayuanan a lja Kadaladalavan.
S.PHON: izua za sə kapajuanan a ʎa kadaɭadaɭavan.
S.TRANSL: There was a person of the Kadaladalavan family at Kapayuanan.

S.FORM: inika sema vavua masengseng ta quma.
S.PHON: inika səma vavua masəŋsəŋ ta quma.
S.FORM: inika sema vavua masengseng ta quma.
S.PHON: inika səma vavua masəŋsəŋ ta quma.
S.TRANSL: She didn’t go away from the village to work in the fields.

S.FORM: tjaɫu tjumaq sakamaya kikutsu sakamaya.
S.PHON: caʎu cumaq sakamaja kikuʦu sakamaja.
S.FORM: tjalju tjumaq sakamaya kikucu sakamaya.
S.PHON: ʦaʎu ʦumaq sakamaja kikuʦu sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: She just stayed at home picking the lice off herself.

S.FORM: kemsa itaita a vat na vaqu.
S.PHON: kəmsa itaita a vat na vaqu.
S.FORM: kemsa itaita a vat na vaqu.
S.PHON: kəmsa itaita a vat na vaqu.
S.TRANSL: She cooked millet one grain at a time.

S.FORM: nu kesain azua ita vat a vaqu, mapeɫuq a pariuk.
S.PHON: nu kəsain azua ita vat a vaqu, mapəʎuq a pariuk.
S.FORM: nu kesain azua ita vat a vaqu, mapeljuq a pariuk.
S.PHON: nu kəsain azua ita vat a vaqu, mapəʎuq a pariuk.
S.TRANSL: When she cooked one grain of millet, it filled the whole wok.

S.FORM: nu kesain a ita makuzan, mapeɫuq a umaq; inika makaya tjumaq a tsautsau.
S.PHON: nu kəsain a ita makuzan, mapəʎuq a umaq; inika makaja cumaq a ʦauʦau.
S.FORM: nu kesain a ita makuzan, mapeljuq a umaq; inika makaya tjumaq a caucau.
S.PHON: nu kəsain a ita makuzan, mapəʎuq a umaq; inika makaja ʦumaq a ʦauʦau.
S.TRANSL: When she cooked a whole ear of millet, it filled the house and people couldn’t get in.

S.FORM: "aitsu inika paḍia," aya ti Kadaladalavan.
S.PHON: "aiʦu inika paɖia," aja ti kadaɭadaɭavan.
S.FORM: "aicu inika padria," aya ti Kadaladalavan.
S.PHON: "aiʦu inika paɖia," aja ti kadaɭadaɭavan.
S.TRANSL: “This is no good,” said the Kadaladalavan family.

S.FORM: "nanguaq a tja tjaulan aitsu.
S.PHON: "naŋuaq a ca cauɭan aiʦu.
S.FORM: "nanguaq a tja tjaulan aicu.
S.PHON: "naŋuaq a ʦa ʦauɭan aiʦu.

S.FORM: ka inika nanguaq a pakaqatsa ta zaɫum," aya pavavala kemsa tu ita vat a vaqu.
S.PHON: ka inika naŋuaq a pakaqaʦa ta zaʎum," aja pavavaɭa kəmsa tu ita vat a vaqu.
S.FORM: ka inika nanguaq a pakaqaca ta zaljum," aya pavavala kemsa tu ita vat a vaqu.
S.PHON: ka inika naŋuaq a pakaqaʦa ta zaʎum," aja pavavaɭa kəmsa tu ita vat a vaqu.
S.TRANSL: Perhaps it would be good to use a lot of water,” they said, and tried cooking one grain of millet.

S.FORM: ini anga ka meliaw.
S.PHON: ini aŋa ka məɭiaw.
S.FORM: ini anga ka meliaw.
S.PHON: ini aŋa ka məɭiaw.

S.FORM: avan anga sika pakaqatsa ta zaɫum, sa pakaliaw a kemsa ta vaqu.
S.PHON: avan aŋa sika pakaqaʦa ta zaʎum, sa pakaɭiaw a kəmsa ta vaqu.
S.FORM: avan anga sika pakaqaca ta zaljum, sa pakaliaw a kemsa ta vaqu.
S.PHON: avan aŋa sika pakaqaʦa ta zaʎum, sa pakaɭiaw a kəmsa ta vaqu.
S.TRANSL: So that is why we use a lot of water, and cook a lot of grains of millet.
